digest.component_or_area: claude-skills shared test-design reference and skill integration
propositions: Create canonical shared/test-design.md TDD standard covering coverage inventory, contract card, tier table, trigger/assert rule, oracle minimum, contrast case, fault realism, gating proof, defect-seeded mutation, and quantified LLM evals with the scale-to-the-bug principle.
Ship byte-identical test-design.md copies in each of rebar-debug, rebar-implement, and rebar-brainstorm skill directories, using real files with no symlinks or machine-specific paths.
Add gating unit test tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py asserting each copy is byte-identical to the canonical file and self-verifying its divergence-detection via a perturbed tmp_path copy.

Integrate the shared file into rebar-debug: retitle Step 4 to RED-oracle design, defer its LLM-surface paragraph to the quantified-eval standard, add the contract card as a fourth fixer handoff item, and make Step 5's mutation check defect-seeded.
Update rebar-implement Phase 4a/4b, rebar-brainstorm's ticket-recording section, and llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md Phase 2 to cite test-design.md's oracle and quantified-eval standards.
All added prompt text addresses the LLM skill user with positive-first guidance, short prohibitions, and no elaborated negative examples, documented via README convention and sync one-liner.
